# X2Chess Architecture Manual

Last updated: 2026-04-20

## 1) Goals

- Make the browser fat client the primary/full product runtime.
- Allow remote launch (server-hosted assets) while preserving local client execution.
- Combine local resource usage with remote resource integration in one client architecture.
- Provide an app-profile variant as a reduced version of the full fat client.
- Keep architecture decisions explicit and traceable.

Architecture decisions are maintained in `doc/architecture-adr-manual.qmd`.

## 2) System Overview

## 2.1 Frontend (`frontend/`)

- Technology: Vite + React 18 + TypeScript (ES modules).
- Main entrypoint: `frontend/src/main.tsx`.
- React state: single `useReducer` store (`AppStoreState`) owned by `AppProvider`; all mutations via `dispatch(action)`.
- Service layer: `useAppStartup` hook initialises all pure-logic services on mount,
  wraps them in a `SessionOrchestrator`, and exposes stable callbacks via `ServiceContext`;
  components consume them with `useServiceContext()`. Each service owns its internal
  state as closure variables; services communicate state changes to React via typed
  callbacks that dispatch fine-grained actions (see §7.3).
- Responsibilities:
  - Render the Game-Viewer (board + PGN editor UI) as React components.
  - Parse PGN into an internal model and serialize back to text.
  - Keep replay/navigation state in React `useReducer`.
  - Manage chess move playback and UI interactions via `ServiceContext` callbacks.
  - Manage multiple open game sessions in parallel tabs.
  - Route load/save through a uniform source adapter contract.

## 2.2 Backend (`backend/`)

- Technology: FastAPI.
- Scope in current architecture:
  - Optional static serving and optional future remote services.
  - Not part of client-local resource loading/saving.
- Constraint:
  - Local runtime resources are owned and handled only by the client runtime.

## 2.3 App Profile Shell (`frontend/src-tauri/`)

- Technology: Tauri 2 (Rust host + web frontend).
- Configuration: `frontend/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`.
- Runtime entrypoint: `frontend/src-tauri/src/main.rs`.
- Capability model:
  - `frontend/src-tauri/capabilities/default.json` with `core:default`, `core:menu:default`.
- Build wiring:
  - Dev URL `http://localhost:5287`.
  - Production frontend assets from `frontend/dist`.

### Desktop menu bar

The native desktop menu bar is defined declaratively in a single TypeScript file:

- **Definition:** `frontend/src/app_shell/menu_definition.ts`
  — a plain, framework-free data structure (`APP_MENU_DEFINITION`) that lists
  every top-level menu and its items (actions, predefined OS items, separators,
  submenus).  Adding, removing, or reordering menu items only requires editing
  this file.

- **Action ids** (`MenuActionId`) name the application-level operations
  (e.g. `"file.open-resource"`, `"file.new-database"`).  Each id is handled by
  a matching entry in the `MENU_ACTIONS` map inside `useTauriMenu.ts`.

- **Build hook:** `frontend/src/hooks/useTauriMenu.ts`
  — a React hook called once by `useAppStartup`.  It reads
  `APP_MENU_DEFINITION`, constructs the Tauri 2 `Menu` via
  `@tauri-apps/api/menu`, wires action callbacks to `AppStartupServices`, and
  installs the result with `menu.setAsAppMenu()`.  The hook is a no-op in
  browser runtime (`isTauriRuntime()` guard).

**To add a new menu action:**
1. Add a new id to `MenuActionId` in `menu_definition.ts`.
2. Insert a `{ kind: "action", id: "…", label: "…", accelerator?: "…" }` node
   at the desired position in `APP_MENU_DEFINITION`.
3. Add the handler `"new.id": (services) => { … }` in `MENU_ACTIONS` inside
   `useTauriMenu.ts`.

## 2.4 Resource Library (`resource/`)

- Technology: TypeScript library consumed by frontend runtime boundaries.
- Scope in current architecture:
  - Defines canonical PGN resource contracts (`PgnResourceRef`, `PgnGameRef`, `PgnGameEntry`).
  - Provides canonical kind model: `file`, `directory`, `db`.
  - Owns metadata schema/constants and extraction contracts.
  - Owns database schema and migrations under `resource/database/`.
- Boundary constraints:
  - Frontend modules consume `resource/client` and `resource/domain` contracts.
  - Frontend must not own DB schema/migration internals.

## 3) Build and Delivery Paths

Environment and toolchain setup is documented in `doc/setup-manual.qmd`.

## 3.1 Full browser fat-client profile
- Dev (local): `cd frontend && npm run dev`
- Build (deployable from remote host or local static host): `cd frontend && npm run build`

## 3.2 Reduced app profile (Tauri)
- Dev shell + hot reload: `cd frontend && npm run desktop:dev`
- Packaged app profile: `cd frontend && npm run desktop:pack`
- Artifact directory: `frontend/src-tauri/target/release/bundle/`

## 3.3 Runtime modes and Developer-Tools

- Build mode is injected as `__X2CHESS_MODE__` (`DEV` or `PROD`).
- Default behavior:
  - `DEV`: Developer-Tools starts ON.
  - `PROD`: Developer-Tools starts OFF.
- Developer-Tools can be toggled in the app menu and persists per user.
- Developer-Tools exposes a bottom fixed dock with tabs:
  - `AST`
  - `DOM`
  - `Raw PGN`
- The dock is user-opened and user-closed; enabling Developer-Tools does not force the dock open.
- Dock views always reflect the same in-memory game/model state as the main Game-Viewer.
- **Raw PGN** tab: users can edit text and apply it via `applyDeveloperDockRawPgn` in the session orchestrator. That path replaces `activeSessionRef` PGN text/model, runs `syncChessParseState`, dispatches a full session snapshot to React, clears per-session undo/redo, updates dirty metadata, and does **not** call `scheduleAutosaveForActiveSession`.
- DEV-only convenience:
  - Automatic preload attempt from `run/DEV/games` (when available).

## 3.4 Game sessions and source adapters

- Open games are modeled as in-memory `GameSession` entries:
  - `sessionId`
  - `sourceRef`
  - `revisionToken`
  - snapshot of editor/board runtime
  - dirty/save mode metadata
- Session identity is independent from source identity; source references are attached metadata.
- Source interactions are routed through canonical resource boundaries:
  - `file` kind: single file containing multiple games
  - `directory` kind: folder of one-game-per-file entries
  - `db` kind: database-backed resources (schema/migrations owned in `resource/database/`; implementation may be deferred by profile)
- Supported open flows are:
  - Drag/drop PGN text/files
  - Paste PGN text
  - Source-root preload (DEV convenience)
- Save path is uniform:
  - Active session + source reference + revision token -> source adapter `save(...)`.
- Save policy:
  - Default mode is autosave.
  - User can switch active-session mode between autosave and manual from the app menu.
- Naming convention:
  - The conjunction of board and text editor is called **Game-Viewer**.

## 3.5 App-Version and Data-Migration Strategy

All persistent data in X2Chess is versioned so that older instances can be detected and
upgraded automatically on startup. Three layers are covered:

### 3.5.1 App version constant

- **Source of truth:** `frontend/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` → injected as `__X2CHESS_APP_VERSION__` by Vite.
- **Access point:** `frontend/src/runtime/app_version.ts` exports `CURRENT_APP_VERSION: string`,
  `parseSemver`, and `isNewerVersion`. All code that needs the version reads from this module;
  no other file duplicates the inline `typeof __X2CHESS_APP_VERSION__` guard.

### 3.5.2 SQLite database schema migrations

- **Owner:** `resource/database/migrations/` (runner + steps + manifest).
- **Mechanism:** `schema_version` table records applied versions. `runMigrations(db)` is idempotent —
  safe to call on every DB open; it skips already-applied steps.
- **Naming convention:** migration files are prefixed `NNNN_` (e.g. `0001_init.ts`).
- **Rule:** Never modify a migration step after it has been shipped. Add a new step instead.

### 3.5.3 localStorage versioned stores

- **Primitive:** `frontend/src/storage/versioned_store.ts` — `createVersionedStore<T>(config)`.
- **Envelope format:** every managed key stores `{ "v": <number>, "data": <T> }` JSON.
- **On read:**
  - Absent key → return default (no write-back).
  - Current version → return data directly.
  - Older version → apply migration chain in-place; write upgraded envelope back.
  - Future version (data from a newer build) → return default *without overwriting*.
  - Corrupt JSON → reset key to default; log a warning.
- **Adding a store:** call `createVersionedStore` with `version: 1` and `migrations: []`.
  Increment `version` and add a migration step whenever the stored shape changes incompatibly.
- **Migration step indexing:** `migrations[i]` upgrades data from version `i` to version `i+1`.
  Array length must equal `version - 1`. A step returning `null` signals unrecoverable data
  and triggers a reset to `defaultValue`.

#### Managed stores (version 1 baseline)

| Store | Key | File |
|---|---|---|
| Shell prefs (compound) | `x2chess.shellPrefs` | `runtime/shell_prefs_store.ts` |
| Shape / board decoration prefs | `x2chess.shapePrefs.v1` | `runtime/shape_prefs.ts` |
| Resource viewer column prefs | `x2chess.resourceViewerColumnPrefs.v1` | `resources_viewer/viewer_utils.ts` |
| Metadata schemas | `x2chess.metadata-schemas` | `resources_viewer/schema_storage.ts` |
| External DB settings | `x2chess.ext-db-settings` | `runtime/ext_database_settings_store.ts` |
| Remote rules cache | `x2chess.webImportRules` | `runtime/remote_rules_store.ts` |
| User web-import rules | `x2chess.webImport.userRules` | `resources/web_import/user_rules_storage.ts` |
| Training badges | `x2chess.training-badges` | `training/transcript_storage.ts` |
| Training sessions | `x2chess.training-sessions` | `training/transcript_storage.ts` |
| Curriculum plan | `x2chess.curriculum-plan` | `training/curriculum/curriculum_storage.ts` |
| Workspace snapshot | `x2chess.workspaceSnapshot` | `runtime/workspace_snapshot_store.ts` |

Dynamic per-tab group-by state (`x2chess.groupby.<tabId>`) and the visual-asset cache
(`chess-app:visual-asset:v2:<key>`) are intentionally excluded — they are ephemeral UI state
and asset caches respectively, not user configuration.

#### Physical storage location

X2Chess is a Tauri v2 desktop app (bundle ID `com.x2chess.app`).  Each platform's
webview engine stores localStorage as opaque binary files — they are **not** standard
SQLite databases and are not intended to be edited directly.

**Default paths** (used when `X2CHESS_WEBVIEW_DATA` is not set):

| Platform | Webview | Path |
|---|---|---|
| macOS | WKWebView | `~/Library/WebKit/com.x2chess.app/WebsiteData/LocalStorage/` |
| Windows | WebView2 (Chromium) | `%APPDATA%\X2Chess\EBWebView\Default\Local Storage\leveldb\` |
| Linux | WebView2 (Chromium) | `$HOME/.local/share/X2Chess/EBWebView/Default/Local Storage/leveldb\` |

On **macOS** each origin maps to one file named `<scheme>_<host>_<port>_0.localstorage`
(WebKit binary format).  The Tauri custom protocol origin is `tauri://localhost`, so all
`x2chess.*` keys share the single file `tauri_localhost_0.localstorage`.

On **Windows / Linux** (WebView2) the storage is a LevelDB directory; individual keys are
not addressable as separate files.

#### Redirecting storage via `X2CHESS_WEBVIEW_DATA`

The Tauri main window is created programmatically in `main.rs` using
`WebviewWindowBuilder::data_directory()`.  Setting the environment variable
`X2CHESS_WEBVIEW_DATA` to any directory path redirects **all** webview storage
(localStorage, IndexedDB, cache, cookies) to that directory.  The directory is created
automatically on first launch if it does not exist.

```bash
# Isolated dev profile — data written to run/DEV/webview-data/ (relative to repo root)
X2CHESS_WEBVIEW_DATA=../run/DEV/webview-data  tauri dev

# Packaged as npm scripts:
npm run desktop:dev:isolated    # isolated (recommended for development)
npm run desktop:dev             # OS default path (shared with any installed PROD build)
```

The directory layout inside a redirected path mirrors the platform default:

| Platform | Sub-path under `X2CHESS_WEBVIEW_DATA` |
|---|---|
| macOS | `WebsiteData/LocalStorage/tauri_localhost_0.localstorage` |
| Windows / Linux | `EBWebView/Default/Local Storage/leveldb/` |

**Full reset of isolated dev storage:**

```bash
rm -rf run/DEV/webview-data   # directory is recreated on next launch
```

> Note: The `x2chess.devPrefsMode` key documented in §4 (dev prefs mode switch) lives in
> the same storage location.  A full reset also clears this flag, so the next launch
> re-initialises it to `"defaults"`.

### 3.5.4 Legacy key consolidation (one-shot migration)

Individual shell-preference keys written by earlier builds (`x2chess.sound`,
`x2chess.moveDelayMs`, `x2chess.locale`, `x2chess.pgnLayout`, `x2chess.developerTools`,
`x2chess.positionPreviewOnHover`, `x2chess.resourceViewerHeightPx`,
`x2chess.boardColumnWidthPx`) are merged into the compound `x2chess.shellPrefs` key on
the **first startup after upgrade** by `migrateLocalStorage()` in
`frontend/src/storage/migrate_local_storage.ts`. The function is idempotent: if the
compound key already exists it returns immediately. Legacy keys are deleted after consolidation.

Similarly, `migrateRemoteRulesCache()` in `runtime/remote_rules_store.ts` merges the
legacy two-key remote rules cache (`x2chess.webImportRules.v1` +
`x2chess.webImportRules.version`) into the compound `x2chess.webImportRules` key.

### 3.5.5 Startup sequence

```
useAppStartup mount effect
  │
  ├─ [SYNC] migrateLocalStorage()         ← consolidate legacy shell-pref keys
  ├─ [SYNC] migrateRemoteRulesCache()     ← consolidate legacy rules-cache keys
  ├─ [SYNC] versioned store reads         ← auto-migrate on first access
  │
  └─ [ASYNC, inside Tauri gateway open]
       runMigrations(db)                  ← idempotent DB schema migration
```

### 3.5.6 Workspace snapshot

The full workspace — all open game sessions and all open resource viewer tabs — is
persisted automatically so it can be restored on the next launch.

**Store:** `x2chess.workspaceSnapshot` (versioned, managed by
`runtime/workspace_snapshot_store.ts`).

**Shape:** `WorkspaceSnapshot` contains:

- `sessions[]` — one `SessionSnap` per open tab: session ID, title, full PGN text
  (including any unsaved edits), `sourceRef`, save mode, dirty state, current ply,
  selected move ID, and PGN layout mode.
- `activeSessionId` — the session that was focused when the snapshot was written.
- `resourceTabs[]` — one `ResourceTabSnap` per open resource viewer tab: kind, locator,
  title.
- `activeResourceTabId` — the active resource tab.

**Write path:** A `useEffect` in `useAppStartup` watches
`state.sessions`, `state.activeSessionId`, `state.resourceViewerTabSnapshots`, and
`state.activeResourceTabId`. Any change schedules a debounced write (500 ms). When
the timer fires, the current snapshots are assembled by calling
`bundle.sessionStore.buildSessionSnapshots()` and `bundle.resourceViewer.buildTabSnapshots()`
and written atomically to the store. Rapid successive changes collapse into a
single write.

**Restore path:** `useAppStartup` reads the snapshot synchronously before opening any
session. When `snapshot.sessions` is non-empty it reconstructs each session from the
stored PGN text and restores the active session pointer and all resource viewer tabs.
When the snapshot is empty (first launch, or after a reset) a blank default session is
opened instead.

**Close-guard:** `AppShell` registers a `beforeunload` handler that calls
`e.preventDefault()` / sets `e.returnValue` whenever any session has `dirtyState ===
"dirty"`. This causes the browser (or Tauri webview) to show the platform's
"unsaved changes — leave anyway?" dialog before the window closes.

### 3.5.7 Version-gating (future)

Neither localStorage nor DB migrations currently require app-version gating (all steps
are additive). If a future migration must only run on a specific version transition, use
`CURRENT_APP_VERSION` / `parseSemver` from `runtime/app_version.ts` as the guard inside
the migration step or `migrateLocalStorage`.

## 4) Configuration

Configuration in X2Chess splits into two distinct categories: **build-time configuration**
embedded at compile time and **user preferences** persisted at runtime.

### 4.1 Build-time configuration

Build-time values are fixed when the application is compiled. They cannot be changed at runtime.

**Source of truth:** `frontend/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`

This file owns:

- `version` — the application semver string (canonical source for `CURRENT_APP_VERSION`;
  see §3.5.1).
- `productName`, `identifier` — product and bundle identity.
- Window dimensions and minimum constraints (`width`, `height`, `minWidth`, `minHeight`).
- Updater endpoint and signing public key.

**Vite build globals**

Vite reads `tauri.conf.json` at build time (`frontend/vite.config.ts`) and injects three
compile-time constants into every bundle:

| Global | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `__X2CHESS_MODE__` | `"DEV"` or `"PROD"` | Controls developer-tools default; consumed by `runtime/bootstrap_prefs.ts`. |
| `__X2CHESS_APP_VERSION__` | Semver string from `tauri.conf.json` | Re-exported exclusively from `runtime/app_version.ts`; used for version-gated migrations. |
| `__X2CHESS_BUILD_TIMESTAMP__` | ISO-8601 string set at build time | Available for diagnostic display. |

All code that needs the version reads it from `runtime/app_version.ts`. No other file reads
`__X2CHESS_APP_VERSION__` directly.

### 4.2 User preferences

User preferences are values the user can change within the application. They persist across
sessions via the versioned localStorage mechanism described in §3.5.3.

Each preference group is a named versioned store backed by a single localStorage key.
Stores are read once on startup (after legacy key consolidation; see §3.5.5) and written
back whenever a preference changes.

#### Preference stores

| Store | Key | File | Contents |
|---|---|---|---|
| Shell prefs | `x2chess.shellPrefs` | `runtime/shell_prefs_store.ts` | Sound, locale, move delay, PGN layout mode, position preview, developer tools, panel sizes |
| Shape / board prefs | `x2chess.shapePrefs.v1` | `runtime/shape_prefs.ts` | Annotation colors, square highlight style, move-hint dots |
| External DB settings | `x2chess.ext-db-settings` | `runtime/ext_database_settings_store.ts` | Opening explorer speed/rating filters |
| Resource viewer column prefs | `x2chess.resourceViewerColumnPrefs.v1` | `resources_viewer/viewer_utils.ts` | Column widths and visibility |

For the complete list of all managed stores see the table in §3.5.3.

#### Preference lifecycle

1. **Startup** — `useAppStartup` reads all stores synchronously; the values are
   dispatched to `AppStoreState` in the mount effect. Service-layer getters
   (`isSoundEnabled()`, `getDelayMs()`, etc.) close over `stateRef`, so they pick up
   the current React state on every call without a separate sync step.
2. **Change** — a user action dispatches through `ServiceContext`. The setter dispatches
   the new value to React and writes the store synchronously (`store.write(value)`).
   Service getters automatically see the new value on the next call via `stateRef`.
3. **Future build** — if a newer build already wrote a higher-versioned envelope, the
   current build returns `defaultValue` without overwriting. The newer data is preserved
   for when that build runs again.

#### Adding a preference

1. Add the field to the relevant `*Prefs` type and to `DEFAULT_*_PREFS`.
2. If the stored shape changes incompatibly, increment `version` and append a
   `MigrationStep` to `migrations`.
3. Expose a setter callback in `ServiceContext` and wire it in `createAppServices.ts`.
4. Register the store in the table in §3.5.3 of this manual.

## 5) Resource Formats

X2Chess handles PGN content through three canonical resource kinds (`file`, `directory`,
`db`) plus a set of import-time converters for external formats. All kinds share the same
adapter interface defined in `resource/domain/contracts.ts`; the resource client in
`resource/client/` routes every operation to the correct adapter by `kind`.

The resource fomat `file` is a single PGN file with multiple games. In this app this format
is treated as read only as writing requires a rewrite of the entire file. Writing ability may
be implemented in the future.

The resource fomat `directory` is a directory of PGN files. It is implemented mainly for 
testing purposes, but it may also serve practical use in situations where a user wishes to
work with games in a storage structure that is independent of database technology.

The resource fomat `db` is an X2Chess database. This type of database is built using SQLite
and is the primary editable resource format. It is a self-contained file that contains all
the data for a single game library. It is the only format that supports the full range of
capabilities of the resource system, including search, filtering, and sorting.

A resource is addressed by a `PgnResourceRef` (`kind` + `locator`). A single game within
a resource is addressed by a `PgnGameRef` (`kind` + `locator` + `recordId`).

### 5.1 PGN file (`file` kind)

- **Locator:** absolute filesystem path to a `.pgn` file.
- **recordId:** 1-based integer position of the game within the file (as a string).
- **Layout:** a single text file containing one or more PGN games concatenated in sequence.
  Games are split on `[Event "…"]` header boundaries.
- **Title derivation:** `Event: White - Black` → `White - Black` → `Event` → `Game N`.
- **Capabilities:** `list` and `load` are implemented. `save` and `create` are deferred
  (the file format does not support partial writes without rewriting the whole file).
- **Implementation:** `resource/adapters/file/file_adapter.ts`.

### 5.2 PGN directory (`directory` kind)

- **Locator:** absolute filesystem path to a folder.
- **recordId:** filename of the individual `.pgn` file within the folder (one game per
  file convention).
- **Layout:** each file in the directory contains exactly one game. The display order and
  any extra metadata that PGN headers cannot carry are stored in a JSON sidecar file
  (`.x2chess-meta.json`) placed next to the directory. The sidecar schema is:

  ```json
  { "version": 1, "games": { "<filename>": { "orderIndex": 0, "extra": {} } } }
  ```

  The sidecar is read on every `list` call and written whenever order or metadata changes.
  A missing or corrupt sidecar is silently treated as empty.
- **Capabilities:** `list`, `load`, `save`, `create`, and `reorder` are all delegated to
  injected callbacks wired by `source_gateway.ts`.
- **Implementation:** `resource/adapters/directory/directory_adapter.ts` +
  `resource/adapters/directory/sidecar.ts`.

### 5.3 X2Chess database (`db` kind)

The `db` kind is the primary editable resource format. It is a SQLite file with a
`.x2chess` extension. One file is one self-contained game library; multiple libraries can
be open simultaneously (one `DbGateway` per file path).

#### 5.3.1 File identity and location

- **Locator:** absolute filesystem path to a `.x2chess` SQLite file.
- **recordId:** UUID string assigned at game creation time.
- New databases are created as empty SQLite files; schema migrations run automatically on
  first access (idempotent runner in `resource/database/migrations/runner.ts`).

#### 5.3.2 Schema

The schema is managed by an ordered migration manifest
(`resource/database/schema/schema_manifest.ts`). Each migration is a version number plus
a list of SQL statements executed in order. Migrations are never modified after shipping;
new steps are always appended.

**`schema_version`** (created by the migration runner itself):

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `version` | INTEGER PK | Applied migration version number |
| `applied_at` | INTEGER | Unix timestamp (ms) when the migration ran |

**`games`** — one row per stored game or position:

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | TEXT PK | UUID assigned at creation |
| `pgn_text` | TEXT | Full PGN text including headers and move tree |
| `title_hint` | TEXT | Fallback display title when PGN headers are absent |
| `created_at` | INTEGER | Unix timestamp (ms) of first insert |
| `updated_at` | INTEGER | Unix timestamp (ms) of last save |
| `order_index` | REAL | Display order; swap-reorder exchanges two values |
| `revision_token` | TEXT | Opaque token updated on every save for conflict detection |
| `kind` | TEXT | `"game"` (default) or `"position"` (detected from `[SetUp "1"]`) |

**`game_metadata`** — denormalized header values for fast search and display:

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `game_id` | TEXT FK → games.id | Parent game (cascades on delete) |
| `meta_key` | TEXT | PGN header key (e.g. `White`, `Black`, `Event`, `Result`) |
| `val_str` | TEXT | String value extracted from the PGN header |

Indexed on `(meta_key)` and `(meta_key, val_str)` for text-search queries.

**`metadata_keys`** — registry of user-defined metadata key names:

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `key` | TEXT PK | Metadata key name |
| `value_type` | TEXT | Value type hint (`"string"` default) |

**`position_hashes`** — position search index (one row per reachable position per game):

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `hash` | TEXT | 16-char hex FNV-1a hash of the normalized FEN (first 4 fields) |
| `game_id` | TEXT FK → games.id | Parent game (cascades on delete) |
| `ply` | INTEGER | Half-move number where this position occurs (0 = start) |

Indexed on `hash` for position-lookup queries. The hash function strips half-move and
full-move counters so transpositions hash identically. The index is rebuilt in full on
every save; the builder is injectable (`BuildPositionIndex`) so the `resource/` package
stays free of chess-engine dependencies.

**`move_edges`** — move frequency index for opening explorer:

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `position_hash` | TEXT | Position hash (same scheme as `position_hashes.hash`) |
| `move_san` | TEXT | Move in SAN notation |
| `move_uci` | TEXT | Move in UCI notation |
| `result` | TEXT | Game result (`1-0`, `0-1`, `1/2-1/2`, `*`) |
| `game_id` | TEXT FK → games.id | Parent game (cascades on delete) |

Indexed on `position_hash`. Used by `explorePosition` to return per-move
frequency/result aggregates for any reachable position across the whole library.

**`training_transcripts`** — per-training-session records (owned by the training module):

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `session_id` | TEXT PK | UUID for the training session |
| `source_game_id` | TEXT | The game being trained against |
| `protocol` | TEXT | Training protocol identifier |
| `started_at` | INTEGER | Unix timestamp (ms) |
| `completed_at` | INTEGER | Unix timestamp (ms), nullable |
| `aborted` | INTEGER | 0 = not aborted, 1 = aborted |
| `config_json` | TEXT | JSON-serialized training configuration |
| `ply_records` | TEXT | JSON array of per-ply training results |
| `annotations` | TEXT | JSON array of post-session annotations |

Indexed on `source_game_id` for per-game transcript queries.

#### 5.3.3 Write path and revision safety

Every `save` call:
1. Optionally checks `revision_token` against the stored value; throws `conflict` on
   mismatch (optimistic concurrency guard).
2. Updates `pgn_text`, `revision_token`, `updated_at`, and `kind` in `games`.
3. Deletes and rebuilds `game_metadata`, `position_hashes`, and `move_edges` for the
   affected game.

Every `create` call assigns a UUID, timestamps the row, appends `order_index` after the
current maximum, and builds all three indexes.

#### 5.3.4 Capabilities

| Operation | Supported |
|---|---|
| `list` | Yes — ordered by `order_index ASC, created_at ASC` |
| `load` | Yes |
| `save` | Yes — with optional revision-token conflict detection |
| `create` | Yes |
| `reorder` | Yes — swaps `order_index` values between two rows |
| `searchByPositionHash` | Yes — queries `position_hashes` |
| `searchByText` | Yes — substring match on `White`, `Black`, `Event`, `Site` |
| `explorePosition` | Yes — aggregated move frequency from `move_edges` |

### 5.4 Import-time format converters

Import converters are one-shot adapters that translate a foreign file format into PGN
strings. They implement `FormatImporter` (`resource/adapters/import/format_import_types.ts`)
and are not resource adapters — they do not implement `list`/`load`/`save`. Converted
games are handed to the caller as PGN text ready to be stored in a `db` or other resource.

| Format | Extensions | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| EPD (Extended Position Description) | `.epd` | Pure TypeScript parser; each line becomes a position-only PGN game with `[SetUp "1"]` and `[FEN "…"]` headers. Opcodes `id`, `bm`, `c0`–`c9`, and `ce` are mapped to PGN headers and comments. |
| ChessBase | `.cbh`, `.cbv` | Delegates binary parsing to the Tauri backend command `import_chessbase_file`; Rust returns an array of PGN strings. The TypeScript layer is complete; the Rust command is deferred. |

**EPD opcode mapping:**

| Opcode | PGN mapping |
|---|---|
| `id` | `[Event "…"]` header |
| `bm` | `[Annotator "bm: …"]` header |
| `c0`–`c9` | Comment block `{ … }` in move text |
| `ce` | Centipawn eval appended to comment: `eval: <value>` |
| All others | Ignored |

## 6) Testing

### 5.1 Test runner and location

Tests use Node's built-in test runner (`node:test`) invoked through `tsx`
for TypeScript support.  No third-party test framework or mocking library is
used; tests rely on real implementations or hand-written stubs.

```bash
# from frontend/
npm test                                       # run all tests
tsx --test "test/foo/**/*.test.ts"             # run a subset
```

Test files live under `frontend/test/`, mirroring the `src/` structure
exactly — for example `src/board/shape_serializer.ts` →
`test/board/shape_serializer.test.ts`.

### 5.2 What to test

**Pure-logic modules are the primary test target.** Any module under `model/`,
`editor/`, `board/`, `game_sessions/`, `resources/`, `resources_viewer/`,
`runtime/`, or `training/` that exports non-trivial logic should have a
corresponding test file.

The `state/app_reducer.ts` reducer and `state/selectors.ts` are pure functions
and are tested like any other pure-logic module, despite living in `state/`.

**React components, hooks, and Tauri gateways are not unit-tested.** They
belong to the integration layer and are validated through manual QA.

### 5.3 Coverage map

| Test directory | Source coverage |
|---|---|
| `test/board/` | `src/board/` — move lookup, navigation, shape parser/serializer, physical board protocols |
| `test/editor/` | `src/editor/` — FEN utils, tree numbering, collapse, editor plan, anchor resolver |
| `test/engines/` | Engine manager, UCI parser/writer/session |
| `test/game_sessions/` | Session store, persistence, ingress handlers |
| `test/model/` | PGN model parser, serializer, commands, move ops, undo/redo, x2-style |
| `test/resources/` | Material key, PGN DB adapter, PGN metadata, web-import rules |
| `test/resources_viewer/` | Annotation parsers: anchor, eval, link, QA, schema storage, todo, train tag |
| `test/runtime/` | Resource ref utilities |
| `test/state/` | App reducer, selectors |
| `test/training/` | Move acceptance, replay protocol, transcript, merge, train-tag parser, curriculum I/O |

### 5.4 Writing new tests

- Import source files with the `.js` extension (required by the ESM/tsx loader):
  `import { foo } from "../../src/bar.js"`.
- Use `String.raw\`…\`` for strings containing backslash sequences (PGN escape
  characters, regex literals) to avoid double-escaping noise.
- Stub external dependencies with minimal inline objects; never duplicate
  production logic in stubs.
- One test file per source module — do not mix multiple modules into one file.

## 7) Key Concepts

### 7.1 GameSessionState and ActiveSessionRef

Each open game tab is backed by a dedicated `GameSessionState` object
(`frontend/src/game_sessions/game_session_state.ts`). This object is the
**working store** for all game-specific mutable state for one session:

- **PGN / model**: `pgnModel`, `pgnText`, `moves`, `verboseMoves`,
  `movePositionById`, `pgnLayoutMode`
- **Navigation**: `currentPly`, `selectedMoveId`, `boardPreview`
- **Editor messages**: `pendingFocusCommentId`
- **Edit history**: `undoStack`, `redoStack`

Fields that are internal to a specific service and never need to cross service
boundaries live as closure-private variables instead of being stored on
`GameSessionState`:

- `animationRunId`, `isAnimating` — owned by the `navigation` closure
- `errorMessage`, `statusMessage` — dispatched directly as `set_error_message`
  / `set_status_message` actions; not stored on the session object

Service modules (`pgn_runtime`, `history`, `move_lookup`, `navigation`,
`pgn_model_update`) never receive a global state object. They receive an
`ActiveSessionRef = { current: GameSessionState }` and read/write only
`sessionRef.current.*`. This makes the active session swappable without
copying data: a session switch is a single pointer assignment
`activeSessionRef.current = target.ownState`. Each `GameSession` in the
session store holds its live `GameSessionState` in an `ownState` field —
there is no freeze/thaw snapshot cycle.

### 7.2 Service-Owned State

Each service manages its own internal state as closure-private variables.
There is no shared mutable `AppState` object.

| Service | Owns |
|---|---|
| `session_store` | `gameSessions[]`, `activeSessionId`, `nextSessionSeq` |
| `resources_viewer` | `resourceViewerTabs[]`, `activeResourceTabId`, metadata-key prefs |
| `session_persistence` | `autosaveTimer`, `saveRequestSeq`, `isHydratingGame`, `defaultSaveMode` |
| `resources` | `gameDirectoryHandle`, paths, `activeSourceKind`, `appConfig`, `playerStore` |
| `navigation` | `animationRunId`, `isAnimating` |
| `history` | (undo/redo stacks are on `GameSessionState` so they survive session switches) |

Services that need to read shell-preference values (sound, locale, move delay)
receive a getter function injected at bundle-creation time: `isSoundEnabled()`,
`getDelayMs()`, etc. These getters close over a `stateRef` maintained in
`useAppStartup` that is updated on every React render, so services always read
the current preference without coupling to React directly.

Shell preferences (sound, locale, move delay, layout mode, position preview,
developer tools) are the authoritative province of the React store
(`AppStoreState`) and are persisted via `shellPrefsStore` — a versioned
localStorage store (`frontend/src/runtime/shell_prefs_store.ts`). Shell
preference setters dispatch to React directly.

### 7.3 The React State Bridge

The React `useReducer` store (`AppStoreState` / `appReducer`) is the
authoritative state for **rendering**. State reaches it through two channels:

#### Service callbacks (automatic)

Services wired in `createAppServices.ts` call typed dispatch callbacks when
their state changes:

| Callback | Action dispatched |
|---|---|
| `onPgnChange` | Full session snapshot: `set_pgn_state` + `set_navigation` + `set_undo_redo_depth` + `set_pending_focus` |
| `onNavigationChange` | `set_navigation` |
| `onUndoRedoDepthChange` | `set_undo_redo_depth` |
| `onStatusChange` | `set_status_message` |
| `onErrorChange` | `set_error_message` |
| `onSessionsChanged` | `set_sessions` |
| `onTabsChanged` | `set_resource_viewer` |

Callbacks close over `dispatchRef` (a mutable ref updated on every render) so
they always use the current React dispatch without recreating service instances.

#### Resource domain events (service -> React subscriptions)

Resource mutations that affect multiple UI surfaces (resource table, collection
explorer, position/text search) are propagated through a typed in-memory event
hub in `frontend/src/core/events/resource_domain_events.ts`.

- Services emit domain facts (`resource.gameCreated`, `resource.gameSaved`,
  `resource.gameReordered`) and the normalized `resource.resourceChanged`.
- Payloads are environment-agnostic (`kind`, `locator`, optional `recordId`);
  service modules do not depend on React or DOM events.
- React hooks/components subscribe with `useEffect` and invalidate/reload only
  matching resources by `kind+locator`.

#### Canonical sync helpers (explicit + shared)

Operations in `SessionOrchestrator` that mutate `activeSessionRef.current`
directly (e.g. `loadPgnText`, `switchSession`, `openPgnText`) call
`flushSessionState()` afterwards. The implementation delegates to shared helpers
in `hooks/session_state_sync.ts`:

- `dispatchSessionStateSnapshot(session, dispatch)` — full sync of PGN +
  navigation + undo/redo depth + pending focus.
- `dispatchNavigationState(sessionLike, dispatch)` — fast path that emits only
  `set_navigation` for high-frequency movement where PGN/editor slices did not
  change.

#### The mutable working store

`activeSessionRef.current` (`GameSessionState`) is a **deliberate mutable
working store** — not a React anti-pattern. Operations mutate it in-place
during a single logical step (e.g. parse PGN, update ply, update selection),
then flush to React once the step is complete. This avoids mid-operation
renders and keeps animation, undo, and board preview coherent.

The pattern is intentional:
- Components **read** from the React store (always consistent).
- Services **write** to `activeSessionRef.current` and flush when done.
- The React store is the display layer; `activeSessionRef.current` is the
  editing scratch pad for the active session.

#### Component contract

React components read state exclusively through selectors (`state/selectors.ts`).
Components trigger mutations exclusively via `ServiceContext` callbacks — they
never call `dispatch` for game logic.

### 7.4 The PGN Text Editor

The PGN text editor is the annotation-aware view of the active game session's PGN
model. It is structured as a pure-logic **render-plan pipeline** that converts the
in-memory PGN model into a flat list of `PlanBlock[]`, which the React render layer
then maps to DOM elements.

#### Layout modes

The editor operates in one of three layout modes, stored per session in
`GameSessionState.pgnLayoutMode`:

| Mode | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `plain` | Raw PGN text; comment content is shown verbatim, no marker processing. |
| `text` | `[[indent]]`/`[[deindent]]` maintain a persistent indentation level for all following comments and moves (including mainline); `[[br]]` becomes a visible line break in the `contentEditable`; comment line breaks follow `EditorStylePrefs.commentLineBreakPolicy` (`always` / `mainline_only`); the first comment before the first move receives *intro styling*. |
| `tree` | One block per variation in DFS order; each non-mainline block opens with a labelled `branch_header` token; `[[indent]]`/`[[deindent]]`/`[[br]]` markers are interpreted (as in text mode), while raw marker text remains in PGN storage; each variation's first comment receives intro styling independently. |

#### Intro section (implicit detection)

When the editor is in **text** or **tree** mode, the *first comment appearing
before the first move of a variation* automatically receives **intro styling** —
a visually distinct block with a configurable left sidebar and tinted
background.  No PGN marker is required: position in the model is the sole
criterion.

Detection inside `buildVariationWalker` (`plan/types.ts`):

```typescript
const applyIntroStyling: boolean = isFirstComment && !state.firstMoveEmitted;
```

- `firstCommentId` — set on the first comment seen in the current scope; a
  later comment in the same variation cannot be the intro.
- `firstMoveEmitted` — set to `true` once any move token has been emitted; a
  comment appearing after the first move is never styled as intro.

In **tree mode** `buildTreeEditorPlan` saves and resets both fields before
processing each variation block, so **each variation's first comment gets intro
styling independently** of the main-line intro.

In **plain mode** `applyIntroStyling` is always `false`; the detection is
skipped entirely and all comments render as plain literal text.

**CSS**: the class `text-editor-comment-intro` is added when
`CommentToken.introStyling === true`.  Visual appearance is driven by the CSS
custom properties `--editor-intro-bg`, `--editor-intro-sidebar-color`, and
`--editor-intro-sidebar-width`, which are set from `EditorStylePrefs` and
default to the shipped design.

> **Note:** The legacy `\intro` PGN marker is gone.  Intro styling is fully
> implicit — no markup is needed in the PGN source.

#### Render-plan pipeline (`editor/plan/`)

`buildTextEditorPlan(pgnModel, { layoutMode, numberingStrategy, commentLineBreakPolicy })` is the single
entry point (re-exported from `editor/text_editor_plan.ts`). It:

1. Allocates a `PlanState` — a cursor over a growing `PlanBlock[]` plus tracking
   fields (`indentDepth`, `firstCommentId`, `firstMoveEmitted`).
2. Delegates to the mode-specific builder:
   - `buildPlainEditorPlan` (`plan/plain_mode.ts`) — uses `buildVariationWalker` with
     a pass-through comment emitter that sets `plainLiteralComment = true`.
   - `buildTextModeEditorPlan` (`plan/text_mode.ts`) — uses `buildVariationWalker` with
     a comment emitter that strips `[[indent]]`/`[[deindent]]` directives, applies
     persistent indentation delta state, converts `[[br]]` to
     `\n`, and calls `nextBlock()` after intro comments.
  - `buildTreeEditorPlan` (`plan/tree_mode.ts`) — has its own DFS traversal;
    comment rendering mirrors text mode marker behaviour (strip `[[indent]]`/`[[deindent]]`,
    apply persistent indentation delta state, render `[[br]]` as newline);
     collects child RAVs inline and emits them as new blocks after the parent
     block's content.
3. Trims leading and trailing empty blocks before returning.

**`buildVariationWalker`** (`plan/types.ts`) — a factory used by plain and text mode.
It returns a mutually-recursive `emitVariation` / `emitMove` pair bound to a
mode-specific `CommentEmitter`. The walker handles comment hoisting (a comment
immediately before a move number is hoisted before that move's token), persistent
indentation state updates from `[[indent]]`/`[[deindent]]`, and `postItems`
ordering from the parsed PGN model.

**Token types** emitted into blocks:

| `tokenType` | Description |
|---|---|
| `move` | A SAN move token; carries `nodeId`, `variationDepth`, `moveSide`. |
| `move_number` | A move-number token (simplified to digit only). |
| `nag` | A NAG glyph (e.g. `!`, `?`, `±`). |
| `result` | The game result (`1-0`, `0-1`, `½-½`, `*`). |
| `space` | A whitespace spacer between tokens. |
| `branch_header` | Tree-mode only; carries the variation label (e.g. `A`, `B.1`). |
| `comment` (kind) | A `CommentToken`; carries `commentId`, `rawText`, display `text`, indent flags, `introStyling`, and `focusFirstCommentAtStart`. |

**Tree variation numbering** (`plan/tree_mode.ts`): each non-mainline block is labelled
by a `VariationNumberingStrategy` — a function from a path array to a string.
The default `alphaNumericPathStrategy` maps the first segment to an uppercase letter
(`A`, `B`, …) and deeper segments to 1-based integers joined by `.` (e.g. `A.1.2`).

#### PGN runtime (`editor/pgn_runtime.ts`)

`createPgnRuntimeCapabilities` owns the lifecycle of the in-session PGN text:

- **`loadPgn()`** — reads the raw PGN text from the editor input element, parses it
  into a model, runs `syncChessParseState`, and calls `onPgnChange` (which
  emits the canonical full session snapshot).
- **`applyPgnModelUpdate(nextModel, focusCommentId?)`** — the primary mutation path
  for model-level edits (commands, move entry). Serializes the new model to text,
  pushes an undo snapshot if the text changed, syncs chess parse state, schedules
  autosave, and calls `onPgnChange` (pending focus is included in that same
  snapshot).
- **`syncChessParseState(source)`** — feeds the PGN text to `chess.js` to populate
  `moves`, `verboseMoves`, and `movePositionById`. Falls back to a stripped
  (annotation-free) copy when annotations cause `chess.js` to reject the input.
  Does not fire any callbacks; callers dispatch state separately.

#### Undo / redo (`editor/history.ts`)

`createEditorHistoryCapabilities` maintains per-session `undoStack` / `redoStack`
(capped at 200 entries) stored on `GameSessionState`. Each entry is an
`EditorSnapshot` — a `structuredClone` of `pgnModel`, `pgnText`, `currentPly`,
`selectedMoveId`, and `pgnLayoutMode`. Undo and redo restore the snapshot and call
`onSyncChessParseState` + `onPgnChange` + `onUndoRedoDepthChange`; they do not go
through `applyPgnModelUpdate` so they do not push further history entries.

### 7.5 The Metadata System

Metadata in X2Chess is a multi-layer system that begins with the PGN text and ends at
search-capable, typed, user-extensible field definitions.

#### PGN headers as source of truth

Every game carries its metadata inside its own PGN text as bracket header lines:

```
[White "Kasparov, G."]
[Result "1-0"]
[WhiteElo "2812"]
[Date "1997.05.11"]
```

The PGN text is always the authoritative record. Derived representations (the DB index,
the viewer display) are computed from it and can be rebuilt at any time.

#### Extraction: SSOT parse, then narrow

Bracket headers are parsed **once** per need into a full string map — no tag is dropped
at parse time:

- **`extractPgnMetadataFromSource(pgnText)`** — reads every unique `[Tag "..."]` line
  (first occurrence per key), fills one `Record<string, string>`, enriches canonical
  `XSqrChessStyle` from legacy style tags when needed, and returns `discoveredKeysInOrder`
  for UI discovery. This is the shared parse used before any subset is taken.

Narrowing (a **view** of the same map — storage on disk is unchanged):

- **`extractPgnMetadata(pgnText, keys?)`** — returns only the requested keys as plain
  strings (default key list: `PGN_STANDARD_METADATA_KEYS`). Use when a caller needs a
  fixed roster (titles, legacy call sites).
- **`mergeMetadataCatalogKeys(discoveredKeys)`** — sorted union of `KNOWN_PGN_METADATA_KEYS`
  and keys seen in the file; used for resource viewer `availableMetadataKeys` on list rows.
- **`extractHybridPgnMetadata(pgnText, keys?)`** — reads through `PGN_METADATA_SCHEMA`
  typed parsers, yielding `HybridPgnMetadata`. Known types:

  | Key | Parsed type |
  |---|---|
  | `Date` | `PgnDateValue` (`{ raw, year, month, day }`) — `?` fields become `null` |
  | `WhiteElo`, `BlackElo` | `number` (via `parseInt`; non-numeric → `undefined`) |
  | `Result` | `PgnResultValue` union (`"1-0" \| "0-1" \| "1/2-1/2" \| "*"`) |
  | `XSqrChessStyle` | `X2StyleValue` union (`"plain" \| "text" \| "tree"`) |
  | `XSqrHead` | `string` — mainline **half-moves only** (move numbers + SAN) through the first branch (see `serializeXsqrHeadMovetext`); written on save |
  | All other known keys | `string` |
  | Unknown keys | `string` (pass-through) |

  Unknown keys are preserved as strings; the `HybridPgnMetadata` type is the intersection
  of `PgnMetadataKnownValues` and `Record<string, PgnMetadataScalar | undefined>`.

#### Key lists

Three ordered key lists define default **narrowing** scopes for typed/hybrid utilities and
the viewer’s default columns (not a limit on what exists in the PGN):

| Constant | Content |
|---|---|
| `PGN_STANDARD_METADATA_KEYS` | 14 keys — the Seven Tag Roster plus ECO, Opening, WhiteElo, BlackElo, TimeControl, Termination, Annotator |
| `KNOWN_PGN_METADATA_KEYS` | Standard roster/supplemental tags plus X2 extensions (`XSqrChessStyle`, `Material`, `XSqrHead`); transitional style header names still parse in `PGN_METADATA_SCHEMA` but are not in this projection list |
| `DEFAULT_RESOURCE_VIEWER_METADATA_KEYS` | 7-key default column set for the resource viewer: White, Black, Date, Event, Result, ECO, Opening (no Source column in defaults; `source` is not offered in the column picker) |

The `METADATA_KEY` const object mirrors `PgnMetadataKnownValues` as string constants so
that renaming a key in the type produces a compile error at every call site.

#### DB-level metadata index

When a game is created or saved in a `db` resource, `writeMetadata` in
`resource/adapters/db/db_indexer.ts` denormalizes header values into the `game_metadata`
table (one row per key–value pair, keyed by `(game_id, meta_key)`). This index drives:

- **List display** — `db_adapter.ts` batch-loads metadata for all games in a single
  query at list time, avoiding per-game reads.
- **Text search** — `searchByText` queries `game_metadata` for substring matches on
  `White`, `Black`, `Event`, and `Site`.

The `metadata_keys` table records which keys are present in a given database; the adapter
returns this list alongside each `list` result so the viewer knows which columns to offer.

Every save deletes and rebuilds the metadata rows for the affected game — the index is
always derived from the current PGN text.

#### Derived `Material` key

For position games (`[SetUp "1"]` present), `writeMetadata` also derives a `Material`
key by passing the `[FEN]` header value through `materialKeyFromFen` in
`resource/domain/material_key.ts`. The key has the form `K<white-pieces>vK<black-pieces>`
with pieces sorted by descending value, e.g. `KQPPPvKRP`, `KBNvK`, `KvK`. It enables
substring search for endgame material configurations.

#### User-defined schemas

Beyond the standard PGN keys, users can define custom metadata schemas. A
`MetadataSchema` contains:

- A stable UUID `id` and a display `name`.
- A monotonically incrementing `version` (bumped on each save).
- An ordered list of `MetadataFieldDefinition` entries, each with a `key`, `label`,
  `type`, `required` flag, `orderIndex` (10, 20, 30, … with gaps for future insertions),
  and optional `selectValues` and `description`.

**Field types:** `text`, `date`, `select`, `number`, `flag`, `game_link`.

Schemas are persisted in localStorage under `x2chess.metadata-schemas` via the versioned
store in `frontend/src/resources_viewer/schema_storage.ts`. They can be exported and
imported as JSON files (format marker: `"x2chess-schema": "1"`).

The built-in `BUILT_IN_SCHEMA` (id `"builtin"`) is a read-only schema for the standard
PGN Seven Tag Roster plus common extensions. It is used when no user-defined schema is
associated with a resource.

#### Relationship between layers

```
PGN text (source of truth)
  │
  ├─ extractPgnMetadataFromSource() → full header map + key order
  │    used by: directory/file list rows, mergeMetadataCatalogKeys, db indexer key set
  │
  ├─ extractPgnMetadata(keys?)    → plain subset map (narrowed)
  │    used by: titles, callers that need only standard tags
  │
  ├─ extractHybridPgnMetadata()   → typed HybridPgnMetadata (narrowed)
  │    used by: resource viewer columns, typed field rendering
  │
  └─ writeMetadata() [db only]   → game_metadata + metadata_keys rows
       used by: text search, batch list loading

MetadataSchema (localStorage)
  └─ maps field keys to types and labels
       used by: resource viewer column configuration, metadata editor dialog
```

## 8) PGN — Format Reference and Glossary

This chapter covers the PGN format as it is used in X2Chess, the terminology
applied throughout the codebase, and a glossary of chess notation terms.

### 8.1 What is PGN?

**Portable Game Notation (PGN)** is the standard plain-text format for
recording chess games.  A PGN file may contain one or more games, each
consisting of two sections:

1. **Tag pair section** — a block of `[Key "Value"]` header lines that carry
   metadata about the game (players, event, date, result, etc.).
2. **Move-text section** — the sequence of moves in SAN, optionally annotated
   with comments, Numeric Annotation Glyphs (NAGs), and nested variations
   (RAVs).

```pgn
[Event "World Chess Championship"]
[Site "New York, USA"]
[Date "1997.05.11"]
[Round "6"]
[White "Deep Blue"]
[Black "Kasparov, Garry"]
[Result "1-0"]

1. e4 e6 2. d4 d5 3. Nc3 Nf6 { A French Defence } 4. Bg5 dxe4
( 4... Be7 $5 { interesting alternative } 5. e5 ) 5. Nxe4 Be7 1-0
```

### 8.2 The Seven Tag Roster

PGN mandates a minimum set of seven header tags, known as the **Seven Tag
Roster (STR)**:

| Tag | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `Event` | Tournament or match name | `"World Chess Championship"` |
| `Site` | Location (city, country) | `"New York, USA"` |
| `Date` | Start date (`YYYY.MM.DD`; unknown fields use `?`) | `"1997.05.11"` |
| `Round` | Round number (or `"?"`) | `"6"` |
| `White` | White player (Last, First) | `"Deep Blue"` |
| `Black` | Black player (Last, First) | `"Kasparov, Garry"` |
| `Result` | Game result: `1-0`, `0-1`, `1/2-1/2`, or `*` | `"1-0"` |

X2Chess extends the STR with additional standard tags such as `WhiteElo`,
`BlackElo`, `ECO`, `Opening`, `TimeControl`, `Termination`, and `Annotator`.
The full canonical list is `PGN_STANDARD_METADATA_KEYS` in
`resource/domain/metadata.ts`.

### 8.3 Move Text and Notation

#### SAN — Standard Algebraic Notation

**SAN (Standard Algebraic Notation)** is the notation used for individual
moves in PGN.  Each move is identified by the piece moved and the destination
square, using the minimum information needed to remove ambiguity.

| Component | Meaning | Example |
|---|---|---|
| No prefix | Pawn move | `e4`, `dxe5` |
| `N`, `B`, `R`, `Q`, `K` | Piece identifier | `Nf3`, `Qd8` |
| `x` | Capture | `Bxe5`, `exd5` |
| `+` | Check | `Qh5+` |
| `#` | Checkmate | `Qf7#` |
| `=` | Promotion piece | `e8=Q` |
| `O-O` | Kingside castling | `O-O` |
| `O-O-O` | Queenside castling | `O-O-O` |

When two pieces of the same type can reach the same square, the file (column
letter) or rank (row number) of the origin is added as a disambiguator, e.g.
`Rfe1` or `N3d2`.

In X2Chess, SAN strings are parsed and replayed using `chess.js`.
`applySanWithFallback` in [board/move_position.ts](../frontend/src/board/move_position.ts)
normalises raw SAN tokens before passing them to `chess.js`, tolerating common
annotation-embedded notation such as `1.e4!` or `3...Nf6?`.

#### UCI — Universal Chess Interface notation

**UCI (Universal Chess Interface)** notation encodes a move as a four- or
five-character string: origin square + destination square (+ promotion piece).
Examples: `e2e4`, `g1f3`, `e7e8q`.  UCI is the format used for communicating
moves with chess engines.  X2Chess stores UCI alongside SAN in the `move_edges`
DB table to support engine-facing interfaces.

#### FEN — Forsyth–Edwards Notation

**FEN (Forsyth–Edwards Notation)** is a compact string that fully describes a
chess position.  A FEN string has six space-separated fields:

```
rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1
│                                              │ │    │ │ └── Full-move number
│                                              │ │    │ └──── Half-move clock (50-move rule)
│                                              │ │    └────── En-passant target square (or -)
│                                              │ └─────────── Castling availability (KQkq or -)
│                                              └───────────── Side to move (w / b)
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Piece placement (rank 8 down to rank 1)
```

Pieces are represented by letters: upper-case for White (`P B N R Q K`),
lower-case for Black (`p b n r q k`).  Digits represent consecutive empty
squares.  Ranks are separated by `/`.

FEN is used throughout X2Chess:
- `[FEN]` and `[SetUp "1"]` headers in PGN mark games that start from a
  non-initial position.
- Every `MovePositionRecord` (built by `buildMovePositionById`) stores the FEN
  after the move, which the board renderer reads to display the correct
  position.
- Position search hashes are derived from a normalised FEN (first four fields
  only, stripping half-move clock and full-move number) so that transpositions
  collide correctly.

### 8.4 Ply, Half-Move, and Full-Move

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Ply** | A single move by one player.  White's first move is ply 1, Black's reply is ply 2, and so on.  Also called a **half-move**. |
| **Half-move** | Synonymous with ply — one player's move in a two-sided exchange. |
| **Full move** | A pair of plies: White's move plus Black's reply.  Full-move counting starts at 1 and increments after Black's move.  Encoded as the sixth field of FEN and as the move-number prefix in PGN (e.g. `3.` means White's third full move). |

In X2Chess the active ply is stored as `currentPly` in `GameSessionState`.
The `MovePositionRecord.mainlinePly` field is a 1-based ply count within the
recorded mainline (not necessarily the chess-clock full-move number, because a
game may begin from a custom FEN).

### 8.5 Variations and RAVs

A **variation (RAV — Recursive Annotation Variation)** is an alternative line
of play embedded in the move text inside parentheses.  Variations can nest
arbitrarily.  The outermost sequence of moves is the **mainline**; every
parenthesised branch is a RAV.

```pgn
1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. Bb5
( 3. Bc4 Bc5 4. c3 ( 4. b4 Bb6 ) 4... Nf6 )
3... a6 4. Ba4
```

The PGN model in X2Chess (`PgnModel`, `PgnVariationNode`, `PgnMoveNode`) is a
recursive tree whose root is the mainline variation.  Each `PgnMoveNode` stores
post-move attachments in `postItems`, where each item is either a comment or a
RAV (`PgnVariationNode`).  Child variations in `postItems` can themselves
contain further nested RAVs through their own move nodes.

The move-position index built by `buildMovePositionById`
([board/move_position.ts](../frontend/src/board/move_position.ts)) records
navigation links (`previousMoveId`, `nextMoveId`, `variationFirstMoveIds`,
`parentMoveId`) so the board can traverse both the mainline and any nested RAV
without rescanning the model tree.

### 8.6 PV — Principal Variation

A **Principal Variation (PV)** is the sequence of best moves found by a chess
engine from a given position.  It is represented as an ordered list of SAN (or
UCI) moves.

In X2Chess, `replayPvToPosition(startFen, pvSans, upToIndex)` in
[board/move_position.ts](../frontend/src/board/move_position.ts) replays a PV
from a starting FEN and returns the resulting FEN, enabling the engine analysis
panel to display the board position at any point along the PV without writing
the moves into the editable PGN model.

### 8.7 NAG — Numeric Annotation Glyph

**NAGs (Numeric Annotation Glyphs)** are a PGN-standard mechanism for
annotating moves and positions with a single integer code prefixed by `$`.  They
replace or supplement the informal `!`, `?`, `!!`, `??`, `!?`, and `?!` symbols
(which are themselves defined as NAG $1–$6 by the PGN standard).

X2Chess defines its supported NAG set in `parts/pgnparser/src/nag_defs.ts`,
divided into three groups:

| Group | Purpose | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| `move_quality` | Quality of the individual move | `$1` `!` Good, `$4` `??` Blunder |
| `evaluation` | Position evaluation after the move | `$10` `=` Equal, `$18` `+-` Winning for White |
| `positional` | Structural or strategic themes | `$20` `⊠` Zugzwang, `$146` `N` Novelty |

Color-specific NAGs (e.g. $32/$33 for initiative, $36/$37 for attack) have a
White and a Black variant; X2Chess selects the correct variant automatically
based on the side to move.

### 8.8 X2Chess PGN Extensions

X2Chess uses a small set of non-standard PGN annotations inside comment blocks.
These follow the convention established by other chess software of embedding
structured data as `[%command arguments]` tokens within `{ }` comments.

| Annotation | Syntax | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Board shapes | `[%csl Ge4,Rf7]` `[%cal Ge2e4]` | Square highlights and arrows on the board |
| Engine eval | `[%eval 0.42]` | Centipawn evaluation from a chess engine |
| Clock | `[%clk 1:25:00]` | Remaining clock time after the move |
| Training tag | `[%train ...]` | Training mode metadata (acceptance signals) |
| Anchor | `[%anchor id="..." text="..."]` | Named position anchor for cross-references |
| Game link | `[%link recordId="..."]` | Reference to another game in the library |

The `XSqrChessStyle` PGN header is also an X2Chess extension (transitional
`XTwoChessStyle` and legacy `X2Style` are still read for backward compatibility):

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `plain` | Display raw PGN text; no marker processing |
| `text` | `[[indent]]`/`[[deindent]]`/`[[br]]` markers active; intro-comment styling applies |
| `tree` | One block per variation in DFS order; each variation's first comment styled independently |

Layout markers (`[[br]]`, `[[indent]]`, `[[deindent]]`) appear inside comment text and control
the visual presentation of the PGN text editor; they have no effect on board
navigation or the underlying move tree.

`EditorStylePrefs` additionally governs comment flow and colours:
- `commentLineBreakPolicy` (`always` / `mainline_only`)
- `variationMoveColor`
- `commentTextColor`

### 8.9 Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Algebraic notation** | See SAN. |
| **Annotation** | Any comment, NAG, or inline extension (`[%...]`) added to a PGN move or position. |
| **Blunder** | A serious mistake by a player; annotated `??` (NAG `$4`). |
| **Book move** | A move found in opening theory; sometimes annotated `N` (NAG `$146`) when it departs from known theory. |
| **CP / Centipawn** | Unit of chess evaluation equal to 1/100 of a pawn.  A positive value favours White; negative favours Black. |
| **ECO** | Encyclopaedia of Chess Openings — a classification system (`A00`–`E99`) encoded in the `ECO` PGN header. |
| **En passant** | A pawn capture in which a pawn that has just advanced two squares can be taken as if it had moved only one.  The target square is stored in FEN field 4. |
| **EPD** | Extended Position Description — a text format for storing one position per line.  Used in X2Chess as an import source (see §5.4). |
| **FEN** | See §8.3. |
| **Full move** | See §8.4. |
| **Game tree** | The complete branching structure of mainline moves plus all RAV variations. |
| **Half-move** | See §8.4 (ply). |
| **Mainline** | The primary sequence of moves in a PGN game, as opposed to RAV variations. |
| **Material key** | An X2Chess-specific encoding of piece material for position games, e.g. `KQPPPvKRP`.  Derived from FEN and stored for endgame search. |
| **Move ID** | A stable, unique string identifier (`moveId`) assigned by the PGN model parser to every `PgnMoveNode` in the game tree; used as the key for board-position lookup and navigation. |
| **Move number** | The full-move counter printed before White's moves in PGN (`1.`, `2.`, …).  Black's reply uses an ellipsis (`1...`). |
| **NAG** | See §8.7. |
| **Novelty** | A new opening move not previously recorded in the literature; annotated `N` (NAG `$146`). |
| **Piece placement** | Field 1 of FEN — the board description in rank-by-rank notation. |
| **Ply** | See §8.4. |
| **Position hash** | A 16-character hex FNV-1a hash of the normalised FEN (first four fields).  Transpositions produce the same hash.  Used in the `position_hashes` and `move_edges` tables. |
| **PGN** | Portable Game Notation — the standard plain-text format for chess games (see §8.1). |
| **PV** | See §8.6. |
| **RAV** | Recursive Annotation Variation — a nested alternative line of play inside parentheses (see §8.5). |
| **Result** | The outcome of the game: `1-0` (White wins), `0-1` (Black wins), `1/2-1/2` (draw), or `*` (game ongoing / unknown). |
| **SAN** | See §8.3. |
| **Seven Tag Roster** | See §8.2. |
| **Tag pair** | A single PGN header line in the form `[Key "Value"]`. |
| **UCI** | See §8.3. |
| **Zugzwang** | A position in which the obligation to move is a disadvantage; annotated `⊠` (NAG `$20`). |

## 9) Constraints for Ongoing Work

- Keep browser fat-client as primary source of feature truth.
- Keep reduced app-profile differences explicit and documented.
- Keep Tauri as reduced app shell, not a replacement for frontend domain logic.
- Keep local resource APIs explicit and maintainable.
- Keep local resource read/write in client code only (no backend mediation).
- Keep remote integrations behind adapter boundaries and contracts.
- Keep top-level `resource/` as canonical home for PGN resource contracts, kind routing, and metadata schema.
- Keep canonical resource kinds explicit as `file`, `directory`, and `db`.
- Keep SQL schema/migration ownership in `resource/database/` (never under frontend domain modules).
- Keep backend optional and decoupled from local resource workflows.
- Enforce Component-Contract on components (integration + configuration + communication APIs, documented publicly in code).
- Enforce clean/professional CSS defaults and maintain explicit theming extension points for future user adaptation.
- Keep local runtime data scenario-separated under `run/`.
- Keep production runtime data outside repository paths (explicit prod root or OS app-data default).
- Keep scenario game libraries under `run/<SCENARIO>/games` for local scenarios.
- Keep runtime configuration layered: default app config + partial user override in data area.
- React `useReducer` (`AppStoreState`) is the authoritative state for rendering; all
  component reads go through selectors, all component writes go through `ServiceContext`.
- `activeSessionRef.current` (`GameSessionState`) is a deliberate mutable working
  store for the active session — services write to it during operations and flush to
  React via callbacks or `flushSessionState()` when the operation completes (see §7.3).
  This is an intentional performance trade-off, not a violation.
- Services own their internal state as closure variables; no shared mutable `AppState` object.
- Keep service initialisation in `useAppStartup`; expose service callbacks exclusively via `ServiceContext`.
- Pure-logic modules (`model/`, `editor/`, `board/`, `game_sessions/`, `resources/`) must remain framework-free.
- Update this manual when architecture decisions, boundaries, or delivery paths change.
